title: Oklahoma AG: State board rigged Jewish charter school rejection
summary: Oklahoma Attorney General Gentner Drummond filed a motion asking a district judge to intervene, alleging state board members rigged their vote to reject a proposed Jewish charter school and seeking legal relief.
angle: State officials rigged vote to block a Jewish charter school — religious group under attack

title: LA taxypayer-funded homeless hotel slammed as ‘boondoggle’ over $625K rooms
summary: Los Angeles purchased a Venice motel during the pandemic to house people experiencing homelessness, but critics are blasting the deal as a taxpayer-funded “boondoggle” after reports suggested the cost worked out to roughly $625,000 per room. City officials and advocates dispute the characterization while opponents use the purchase to fuel outrage over government spending and accountability.
angle: taxpayer-funded boondoggle — outrage over costly homeless hotel purchase

title: First Trump Scrapped “DEI” Coins. Now the New Dime Is Losing the Olive Branch.
summary: Mother Jones reports that the Trump administration has moved to alter U.S. Mint designs — earlier scrapping DEI-themed coins and now approving a new dime that removes the traditional olive branch — framing the changes as symbolic revisionism in the culture-war over national values.

title: Islamic schools, more parents sue Texas over exclusion from voucher program
summary: Islamic schools and parents filed a lawsuit against Texas officials alleging that Muslim schools were improperly excluded from a state school voucher program, claiming religious discrimination.

title: Taylor Tomlinson’s Netflix special is too ungodly for many churches. This one welcomed her.
summary: Fountain Street Church, known for rejecting dogma and pushing boundaries, hosted formerly religious comedian Taylor Tomlinson to film her Netflix special in November despite criticism from other churches deeming the material too ungodly.

title: A grim list: Some notable attacks on houses of worship around the world in recent years
summary: A roundup of notable attacks on houses of worship worldwide in recent years, noting that an ambush at a major U.S. synagogue has intensified fear among clergy and worshippers.

title: French mayoral elections gauge far-right strength before presidential ballot
summary: French voters went to the polls to elect mayors in a vote widely viewed as a test of the far-right's strength and the resilience of mainstream parties ahead of next year's presidential election.
